title: Fashion and the Environment
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The fashion industry is (0) largely responsible for some of the most serious environmental damage caused by consumer culture. The (17) production of new garments requires enormous amounts of water, energy, and raw materials. Many dyes used in manufacturing are highly (18) damaging to rivers and ecosystems. Western consumers have developed a culture of excessive (19) consumption, buying cheap clothes and discarding them quickly. Growing (20) awareness of these issues has led many shoppers to seek out ethical brands. Despite progress, some companies continue to behave in an (21) irresponsible manner, making false claims about their environmental practices. Truly (22) ethical fashion requires transparency throughout the entire supply chain. Clothes made from natural fibres can be (23) beautiful and long-lasting, reducing the need for frequent replacement. Experts now argue that fast fashion is simply (24) unfashionable for a society that takes environmental issues seriously.

example.text: The fashion industry is (0) LARGELY responsible for some of the most serious environmental damage caused by consumer culture. (LARGE)

questions.explanation: A noun is needed after 'The'; 'produce' becomes 'production' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
An adjective is needed after 'highly'; 'damage' becomes 'damaging' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
A noun is needed after 'excessive'; 'consume' becomes 'consumption' by replacing '-me' with '-mption'.
A noun is needed after 'Growing'; 'aware' becomes 'awareness' by adding the suffix '-ness'.
A negative adjective is needed; the prefix 'ir-' is added to 'responsible' to mean 'not acting carefully'.
An adjective is needed before 'fashion'; 'ethics' becomes 'ethical' by adding the suffix '-al'.
An adjective is needed after the verb 'be'; 'beauty' becomes 'beautiful' by changing '-ty' to '-tiful'.
A negative adjective is needed; 'fashion' → 'fashionable', then the prefix 'un-' creates 'unfashionable', meaning 'no longer popular or acceptable'.
